reasoning: A vaccine for a disease takes years to develop, undergo clinical trials, and receive regulatory approval. Currently, there is no approved human vaccine for Pasteurellosis (which is typically treated with antibiotics). The forecast horizon is extremely short (resolving on April 5, 2026, just one week from the forecast date of March 29, 2026). Given that the Wikipedia page stated "No" as of March 19, 2026, it is practically impossible for a new vaccine to be developed, approved, and subsequently updated on Wikipedia within this brief timeframe. Thus, the probability of the value being 'Yes' on the resolution date is as low as permitted.
